title: 8BIT
subject: The intersections of video games, art, and music

title: Chasing Ghosts: Beyond the Arcade
subject: The golden age of video arcade games

title: The King of Kong: A Fistful of Quarters
subject: The rivalry between Billy Mitchell and Steve Wiebe over the Donkey Kong record high score

title: Second Skin
subject: Follows seven people through the world of MMORPGs

title: Ecstasy of Order: The Tetris Masters
subject: A documentary following world-record holding Tetris players as they prepare for the 2010 Championships

title: Indie Game: The Movie
subject: Documentary about the struggles of independent game developers Edmund McMillen and Tommy Refenes during the development of Super Meat Boy, Phil Fish during the development of Fez, and also Jonathan Blow, who reflects on the success of Braid.

title: Thank You for Playing
subject: Follows the creation of the arthouse video game That Dragon, Cancer

title: The Lost Arcade
subject: About the influence of the Chinatown Fair arcade on the fighting game community and New York City as a whole.

title: Moleman 4 – Longplay
subject: A documentary which recounts the so far little-known story of the beginnings of video game development behind the Iron Curtain. Outfoxed Nintendo, surprised Commodore engineers, The Last Ninja story, a games software outfit that dodged the limelight and led the world.

title: Polybius: The Video Game That Doesn't Exist
subject: About the urban legend of Polybius.

title: League of Legends Origins
subject: Fans, experts and creators of the League of Legends detail the game's rise from free demo to global esports titan.

title: Uncle Art
subject: The story of the man behind some of the most memorable gaming music of the '80s and '90s – Starglider, Carrier Command, Beneath a Steel Sky, After Burner and more:  Dave Lowe.

title: Cannon Arm and the Arcade Quest
subject: Following the attempts of Kim "Cannon Arm" Kobke to break the world record of arcade game Gyruss.

title: Games Odyssey
subject: German four-part documentary about the history of video games, simulations, digital adventures and video games as an art form

title: Game Makers
subject: Series on video game industry figures

title: Tetris: From Russia With Love
subject: History of the 1980s Tetris game phenomenon

title: Game On!: The Unauthorized History of Videogames
subject: The story of the video games industry at the Wii and PlayStation 3 console launches

title: Cyberdreams/Cyberdrømme
subject: Danish documentary about the national eSports team competing in World Cyber Games

title: How Videogames Changed the World
subject: Charlie Brooker explores the history of interactive entertainment and how it is changing how we work, communicate and play

title: The Gamechangers
subject: The story of the controversy caused by Grand Theft Auto, a video game series by Rockstar Games, as various attempts were made to halt the production of the games.

title: Game Over: Gender, Race & Violence in Video Games
subject: An educational documentary that explores the messages video games send about sex, race, gender, and violence.

title: Once Upon Atari
subject: Documentary about the rise and fall of Atari, with behind the scenes input from video game designers and programmers who worked there.

title: Video Game Invasion: The History of a Global Obsession
subject: Documentary about the history of video games.

title: It's in the Game
subject: The game industry, technology, and the future of gaming.

title: Beyond the Game
subject: About the world of professional video gaming.

title: Playing Columbine
subject: Following the video game Super Columbine Massacre RPG! in which players experience the Columbine High School massacre through the eyes of the murderers, Eric Harris and Dylan Klebold.

title: Moral Kombat
subject: A study of video game violence.

title: Get Lamp
subject: Documentary by historian Jason Scott about interactive fiction (text adventures) and Infocom.

title: King of Chinatown
subject: Following professional esports player Justin Wong as he and his manager TriForce, attempt to make a career in the competitive Street Fighter IV scene.

title: Level Up - A story about gamers and the games they play
subject: Exploration of video gaming culture.

title: 100 Yen: The Japanese Arcade Experience
subject: Historical documentary about the evolution of Japanese arcades and the culture surrounding it.

title: Minecraft: The Story of Mojang
subject: Documentary about the history of the company Mojang and its creation, Minecraft.

title: The Smash Brothers
subject: A documentary series that covers the early years of the Super Smash Bros. Melee competitive scene, focusing on the lives and subsequent story-lines of seven of the game's most dominant players at the time.

title: The Art of the Game
subject: Documentary that focuses on Academy of Art University students as they compete for a position in the game industry.

title: Atari: Game Over
subject: Documentary on the Atari video game burial excavation.

title: Free to Play
subject: Documentary by video game developer Valve about the lives of three players competing in a gaming tournament for Dota 2.

title: From Bedrooms to Billions
subject: Documentary telling the story of the British video games industry from 1979 to the present day.

title: Good Game
subject: Nine men pursue careers in competitive video games as members of the Evil Geniuses' StarCraft II division.

title: Gaming in Color
subject: Documentary on the LGBTQ community in video games.

title: The King of Arcades
subject: Following classic arcade collector Richie Knucklez as he opens an arcade business in Flemington, New Jersey.

title: Video Games: The Movie
subject: Documentary about the history of video games.

title: GameLoading – Rise of the Indies
subject: Follows several independent game developers, including Davey Wreden (The Stanley Parable), Rami Ismail (Luftrausers) and Zoë Quinn (Depression Quest).

title: GTFO
subject: Documentary about sexism and women in the world of video games.

title: Man vs Snake: The Long and Twisted Tale of Nibbler
subject: Following players as they try to accumulate a billion points on the 1982 arcade game Nibbler.

title: Nintendo Quest
subject: Documentary road film about Jay Bartlett and his quest to acquire all 678 licensed Nintendo Entertainment System games within the span of 30 days, without purchasing any games online.

title: Beep: A Documentary History of Game Sound
subject: Examining the history of game sound design from penny arcades, pinball and video games up to 2015.

title: FGC: Rise of the Fighting Game Community
subject: Documentary about the fighting game community.

title: From Bedrooms to Billions: The Amiga Years!
subject: Documentary on the Amiga and how it influenced a generation of game developers.

title: Console Wars
subject: Documentary about the 1990s console wars between Nintendo and Sega in the 16-bit era and the rise and fall of Sega in the home console market.

title: From Bedrooms to Billions: The PlayStation Revolution
subject: Documentary on the creation of the Sony PlayStation and its successor consoles.

title: Insert Coin
subject: Documentary on the history of Midway Games.

title: Metagame
subject: A follow-up to 2013's The Smash Brothers, this documentary series focuses on the events that led up to the rise of the Five Gods of Melee, and their subsequent downfall and defeat at the hands of William "Leffen" Hjelte.

title: Rarity: Retro Video Game Collecting in the Modern Era
subject: Documentary on video game collecting.

title: Power On: The Story of Xbox
subject: Documentary of the story of the creation, evolution, challenges and legacy of Microsoft Xbox consoles after 20 years of its original launch, divided on 6 chapters.

title: Stamps Back
subject: The story of how teenagers in Hungary ignited a computing revolution in the 1980s with illegally copied video games from the West, and began the Hungarian demoscene.

title: We Met in Virtual Reality
subject: Documentary that takes place entirely within the video game VRChat, exploring the social relations developed by users and how their lives were changed by their time on the platform.

title: Running With Speed
subject: Documentary about the speedrunning community narrated by speedrunning historian Summoning Salt.

title: FPS: First Person Shooter
subject: Bringing together the largest ensemble of gaming icons ever assembled on screen, FPS: First Person Shooter takes fans on a nostalgic journey through classics from Doom and Duke Nukem 3D to GoldenEye 007, Halo, and beyond.

title: Grand Theft Hamlet
subject: Documentary about a production of William Shakespeare's Hamlet delivered entirely in Grand Theft Auto Online during the COVID-19 lockdown in England in 2020.

title: George A. Romero's Resident Evil
subject: About the unrealized film adaptation of the horror video game series Resident Evil, for which filmmaker George A. Romero was considered as a director.
